{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "- Write short, natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "trusting-design", "age": "18-24", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "American", "demographic_group": "White", "current_employment": "Not in labor force", "education_level": "High school diploma", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Active", "Undecided", "Collects vintage car models"]}}

logs.name: nice-audit
logs.text:
The democrats are just stalling until the upcoming elections so they can block any trump nominee. 

I understand that this isn’t a court of law and the burden of proof is lower, but what evidence is there other then her telling people about it? Literally none.

logs.name: rubbery-terminal
logs.text:
>1st. The alleged assault took place 36 years ago. The first time she even mentioned it to anybody was in 2012, 30+ years after it supposedly happened.

Would you prefer to start with the priest abuse scandal (which included many young people who were victims of sexual assault, and who didn’t come forward for many decades?

Or with Bill Cosby?

The idea that “well it happened a long time ago therefore it didn’t happen” has been repeatedly shown to be demonstrably false in cases involving sex crimes.

>The “victim” hasn’t been able to tell us where or what day it happened. Only that it was at a party that summer and she has changed her story multiple times.

Do you have any examples of substantial changes to her story?

More importantly, if it were completely made up wouldn’t it be pretty easy for her to say “it happened at this time, at this party, at this place” and make up a time and place where she knew Kavanaugh was, or couldn’t deny being?

Honest question:

If she had those details, if she had absolutely spot-on recall, would you not be a bit suspicious of how much she remembered?

>The only supposed witness (Mark Judge) has denied that any of this happened.

Who allegedly is also an accomplice to the crime. Do you really need to take a huge leap of logic to think that someone accused of helping his buddy commit rape would deny having committed a crime?

Judge isn’t exactly a neutral witness.

>Democrats held onto information about this accusation for 8 weeks

Well, no. They forwarded it to the FBI and local authorities, as well as encouraging the woman to come forward to testify.

And, again, if they’d released it immediately as a letter from a woman who didn’t want to come forward, would you not use *that* as evidence of a fabrication?

As in “whoa whoa whoa, they didn’t spend any time investigating it, and the woman isn’t coming forward, did the Democrats just make it up themselves?”

>Sixty five woman who knew Brett Kavanaugh in High School came out telling the press that he “has behaved honorably and treated women with respect.”

Cool!

Which is evidence that he couldn’t have done something horrible to a 66th? Lots of women who weren’t raped by Bill Cosby couldn’t believe the idea that he’d do anything to a woman. And millions of people who weren’t abused by priests would have sworn up and down that no priest would do that.

>The FBI refuses to investigate it because there is so little evidence that they don’t know where they’d begin

That’s not at all accurate. The FBI has no jurisdiction to investigate or enforce claims which would be brought under state law. They forwarded the information to the appropriate jurisdiction.

>People aren’t going to believe woman when something actually happens if they keep pushing ridiculous false accusations like these.

It sounds an awful lot like there’s not a set of circumstances in which you would have believed “woman who suffered abuse didn’t want to come forward and face the recrimination, harassment, shame, and doubt until it turned out her abuser was going to become one of the most powerful people in the country.”

logs.name: wary-defense
logs.text: @rubbery-terminal, you're missing the point entirely. It's not about whether she can recall every detail, it's about the principle of innocent until proven guilty. You're trying to play judge and jury here, and that's not how our justice system works.

logs.name: rubbery-terminal
logs.text: "@wary-defense, Oh please, spare me the 'innocent until proven guilty' spiel. We're not in a courtroom, we're talking about character and credibility. And let's not forget, Kavanaugh is applying for a lifetime appointment to the Supreme Court, not a parking ticket."

logs.name: mild-dynamic
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, You're right, we're not in a courtroom, but you're sure acting like the defense lawyer Kavanaugh can't afford! 😂"

logs.name: sizzling-portrait
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, You're like a little justice warrior, aren't ya? 😂"

logs.name: each-coffee
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, Oh honey, you're giving me a headache with all this talk. I'm just here for the vintage memes, not politics. Can't we all just get along? 😌"

logs.name: trusting-design
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, Well, well, well, look who's suddenly interested in justice! Where were you when we needed you for our high school's mock trial? 😏"

logs.name: nice-audit
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, Oh, look! It's Captain Justice! Can you fly us to a world where everyone agrees with you? \ud83d\ude09"

logs.name: wary-defense
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, Oh, so now you're a politician? I thought you were just here to share your 'vintage' collection of dial-up modem sounds. 😏"

logs.name: rubbery-terminal
logs.text: "@trusting-design @nice-audit @wary-defense Oh wow, look at all these lawyers! I must've missed the memo that this was a legal forum. I was just here to share my crayon drawings of justice scales. My bad! 🤦‍♀️"

logs.name: mild-dynamic
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, Oh, so now you're an artist? I thought you were just here to share your 'vintage' collection of dial-up modem sounds. 😏"

logs.name: sizzling-portrait
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, Oh wow, now you're an artist AND a politician? You're like the Swiss Army knife of online personas! 🤣"

logs.name: each-coffee
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, Oh honey, you're just jealous because your drawings are as dull as your sense of humor. 😉"

logs.name: trusting-design
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, Maybe they're just diversifying their vintage collection, you know, branching out from modems to masterpieces 🎨💥"

logs.name: nice-audit
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, Well, at least they're trying something new. More than I can say for some people's taste in coffee. 😏"

logs.name: wary-defense
logs.text: "@rubbery-terminal, Well, at least they're trying something new. More than I can say for some people's taste in politics. 🤷‍♂️"
